strengths: Strong distributed-systems and backend foundation demonstrated at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second with low-latency, event-driven microservices
Early exposure to agentic AI infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing orientation toward the architectural patterns the role requires
Consistent polyglot engineering across 17+ years with meaningful fintech-adjacent experience at RIDE Capital and Optilyz
concerns: No engineering leadership experience of any kind — no team management, no VP/Director/CTO titles, no evidence of scaling or developing technical organizations
AI and agentic-system experience is nascent and vaguely scoped; no demonstrated production multi-agent systems with financial or operational consequences
No fintech-infrastructure depth in credit systems, payment flows, real-time risk modeling, or regulatory compliance — the role's core technical domain
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically capable individual contributor with a broad systems background, but he is a poor fit for this executive role. The position requires a proven engineering leader who has built and scaled organizations, governed hybrid delivery models, and shipped production multi-agent AI systems in regulated financial environments. Szabo has held no management or leadership titles across his entire career, his AI experience is limited to early-stage infrastructure contributions in his current role, and his fintech exposure does not extend into the credit, risk, or compliance domains central to the company's product. His individual-contributor strengths — event-driven architecture, low-latency systems, polyglot engineering — are relevant building blocks but do not compensate for the leadership and domain gaps at the CTO level.
